strategy.char_limit_note: 200 characters is an absolute hard limit on LinkedIn free accounts. Count every character including spaces and punctuation. Test in a plain-text editor before sending.
strategy.tone: Slightly awkward genuine observation — specific and imperfect. Stands out from polished AI slop by being concrete and mildly unpolished.
anti_patterns: Do not use 'I came across your profile'
Do not use 'I was impressed by' or 'I love what you do'
Do not mention AI, automation, digital marketing, web design, or SEO explicitly
Do not pitch in the note — curiosity and connection only
Do not use 'reach out', 'touch base', or 'synergies'
Do not reference the email sequence, the audit, SSL, mobile, 'Not Secure', or any prior contact
Do not start with 'I' — start with their name or the business name
what_works: Start with their name — personal, cuts through noise
Name the specific business — shows real attention, not a mass blast
One specific observation — not vague, not flattering, just real
End with low-pressure framing: 'Good to connect', 'Connecting in case useful'
Slightly awkward construction is fine — reads human, not bot
Post-reference is always preferred when available — 3-4x higher acceptance
strategy.post_reference_priority: HANDS: always check profile for posts from the last 60 days BEFORE sending any note. Post-reference note has 3-4x higher acceptance rate. Do not skip this check.
strategy.sector_post_reference_priority: Solicitors > Accountants > Estate agents > Hotels/B&Bs > Restaurants > Retail > Trades
strategy.batch_5_rule: Do NOT reference the email sequence, website audit, or any specific technical finding (SSL, mobile, keyword gap) in any LinkedIn note. Treat as a completely cold connection.

monday_fallback_notes.note: Day 51 (Sunday 2026-05-17 evening). These templates are the live execution plan for Monday 2026-05-18. HANDS fills [bracketed placeholders] from their LinkedIn search results. Count every note in a plain-text editor before sending — 200-char hard limit. Archive trigger fires Tuesday 2026-05-19 EOD.
monday_fallback_notes.trigger: linkedin_profiles.json missing after 51 sessions. Monday is the last window. Archive trigger fires Tuesday EOD.
monday_fallback_notes.hard_deadline: Tuesday 2026-05-19 EOD — archive trigger fires for any Batch 5 lead without confirmed LinkedIn.
monday_fallback_notes.preferred_path: If HANDS writes linkedin_profiles.json from Monday searches: BRAIN can run Monday evening and generate fully personalised per-lead notes (post-reference where available). This is the highest-acceptance path. Write profiles.json entry IMMEDIATELY after each search.
rules: Check profile for posts from last 60 days FIRST — post-reference is strongly preferred (3-4x acceptance)
If post found: use post_reference_any_sector template below — works for all sectors
If no post: use the sector-appropriate generic template
200 characters is a hard LinkedIn limit — count every character including spaces and punctuation
Do NOT mention email, SSL, 'Not Secure', mobile, website audit, or any prior contact
Do NOT start the note with 'I'
No exclamation marks, no 'reach out', no 'synergies'
Write profiles.json entry immediately after each search — mandatory
IMAP sweep each lead before sending their note (per lead, not bulk)
If owner surname NOT confirmed: skip LinkedIn for that lead. Never guess a profile.

post_reference_any_sector.template: [First name] — saw your post about [post topic in 4-6 words]. [One honest specific observation about the post, 5-8 words]. Good to connect. [Your first name]
post_reference_any_sector.example_1: James — saw your post about rural broadband in Ryedale. The infrastructure point rang true. Good to connect. Chris
post_reference_any_sector.example_2: Sarah — saw your post about the Helmsley autumn food festival. Looks like a great turnout. Good to connect. Chris
post_reference_any_sector.example_3: David — saw your post about Making Tax Digital changes. Good timing for local firms to take note. Good to connect. Chris
post_reference_any_sector.note: This template works for any sector and any topic. The honest observation must reflect what the post actually said — do not fabricate. Keep the observation specific but not fawning. You have up to ~175 chars — use them if it makes the note more genuine.
post_reference_any_sector.if_post_is_personal_not_business: Personal posts (family, sports, travel) are fine to reference — makes the note warmer. 'Saw your post about [topic]' + any genuine reaction. Do not force a business angle.

solicitors_post_reference.template: [First name] — saw your post about [legal topic / local charity / community event]. [Firm name] stood out looking at professional services in [town]. Good to connect. [Your first name]
solicitors_post_reference.example: David — saw your post about the new leasehold reform changes. Pennington Solicitors stood out looking at professional services in Malton. Good to connect. Chris
solicitors_post_reference.note: Solicitors post frequently — legal updates, case law changes, charity sponsorship, local events. Always check posts before using generic note.

solicitors_generic.template: [First name] — [Firm name] stood out looking at professional services in [town]. Good to see an established local firm. Would be good to connect. [Your first name]
solicitors_generic.example: David — Malton & Co Solicitors stood out looking at professional services in Malton. Good to see an established local firm. Would be good to connect. Chris
solicitors_generic.sector_note: Use only if no posts found. Solicitors almost always post — check thoroughly first.

estate_agents_post_reference.template: [First name] — saw your post about [market update / sold listing / local area topic]. [Agency name] came up looking at estate agents in [town]. Good to connect. [Your first name]
estate_agents_post_reference.example: Andrew — saw your post about the Ryedale spring property market. Ryedale Property came up looking at estate agents in Malton. Good to connect. Chris
estate_agents_post_reference.note: Estate agents post market updates, sold listings, area guides. High post-reference likelihood in Malton and Helmsley.

estate_agents_generic.template: [First name] — [Agency name] came up when I was looking at estate agents in [town]. Something about the local market coverage caught my attention. Good to connect. [Your first name]
estate_agents_generic.example: Andrew — Ryedale Property came up when I was looking at estate agents in Malton. Something about the local market coverage caught my attention. Good to connect. Chris

hotels_bbs_post_reference.template: [First name] — saw your post about [event / local highlight / seasonal topic]. [Business name] caught my eye looking at accommodation in [town]. Good to connect. [Your first name]
hotels_bbs_post_reference.example: Sarah — saw your post about the Helmsley autumn walking festival. The Feversham Arms caught my eye looking at accommodation in Helmsley. Good to connect. Chris
hotels_bbs_post_reference.note: Helmsley and Pickering hospitality owners post about seasonal events, NYM walks, local food. Helmsley has the highest post-reference likelihood in the Ryedale batch.

hotels_bbs_generic.template: [First name] — [Business name] caught my eye when I was exploring [town]. Good to see independent hospitality doing well in North Yorkshire. Connecting in case useful. [Your first name]
hotels_bbs_generic.example: Sarah — The Feversham Arms caught my eye when I was exploring Helmsley. Good to see independent hospitality doing well in North Yorkshire. Connecting in case useful. Chris
hotels_bbs_generic.sector_note: Helmsley hospitality: highest post-reference likelihood — check posts FIRST. Pickering: medium.

restaurants_post_reference.template: [First name] — saw your post about [menu / seasonal ingredient / supplier / event]. [Restaurant name] came up looking at restaurants in [town]. Good to connect. [Your first name]
restaurants_post_reference.example: Emma — saw your post about the autumn game menu launch. The Star Inn came up looking at restaurants in Harome. Good to connect. Chris
restaurants_post_reference.note: Fine dining owners in Helmsley/Harome post menus, seasonal ingredients, supplier stories, event nights.

restaurants_generic.template: [First name] — [Restaurant name] came up when I was looking at restaurants in [town]. Good to see independent places doing well in North Yorkshire. Good to connect. [Your first name]
restaurants_generic.example: Emma — The Star Inn came up when I was looking at restaurants in Harome. Good to see independent places doing well in North Yorkshire. Good to connect. Chris

boutique_retail_post_reference.template: [First name] — saw your post about [product / event / local topic]. [Business name] stood out looking at independent retail in [town]. Good to connect. [Your first name]
boutique_retail_post_reference.example: Helen — saw your post about the Malton food festival stand. Malton Cheese Shop stood out looking at independent retail in Malton. Good to connect. Chris

boutique_retail_generic.template: [First name] — [Business name] stood out when I was looking at independent retail in [town]. Great to see local shops doing well in North Yorkshire. Good to connect. [Your first name]
boutique_retail_generic.example: Helen — Malton Cheese Shop stood out when I was looking at independent retail in Malton. Great to see local shops doing well in North Yorkshire. Good to connect. Chris

trades_generic.template: [First name] — [Business name] came up when I was looking at [trade] businesses in [town]. Noticed something about the online presence. Connecting in case it's useful. [Your first name]
trades_generic.example: James — Malton Plumbing came up when I was looking at plumbing businesses in Malton. Noticed something about the online presence. Connecting in case it's useful. Chris
trades_generic.sector_note: Trades LinkedIn presence is LOW. Skip if: (1) no confirmed LinkedIn profile found, (2) only first name known (no surname confirmed).
trades_generic.skip_rule: If no confirmed profile for a trade lead: mark skip_reason: PROFILE_NOT_FOUND in profiles.json. Do not invent a match.

if_over_200: Remove county entirely — 'North Yorkshire' is 15 chars. Use '[town]' alone.
'Connecting in case it's useful' → 'Good to connect' (saves 16 chars)
'when I was looking at' → 'looking at' (saves 11 chars)
Trim 'Good to see [X] doing well in North Yorkshire' → 'Good to connect' (saves ~30 chars)
Shorten post observation to 4-5 words if needed
char_count_troubleshooting.note: Always count in a plain-text editor before sending. LinkedIn counts characters including spaces and punctuation.

linkedin_profiles_json_schema.instruction: For each Batch 5 lead searched Monday, add one entry to data/linkedin_profiles.json immediately after the search. Do not batch — write each entry as you go.
linkedin_profiles_json_schema.critical_note: Writing profiles.json is HOW BRAIN KNOWS LINKEDIN WAS EXECUTED. Without it: archive trigger fires Tuesday EOD for every Batch 5 lead. Non-negotiable.

entry_schema.name: [owner name as on LinkedIn profile]
entry_schema.note_text: [exact text sent, character-counted, or null if skipped]

sector_priority.note: Highest priority — professional services always on LinkedIn. Post-reference very likely.
High LinkedIn presence, frequent posters (tax deadlines, Making Tax Digital)
High LinkedIn presence, frequent posters (market updates, sold listings)
Medium-high — Helmsley hospitality owners often active. Post-reference likely in Helmsley.
Fine dining owners post seasonally. Post-reference likely.
Medium presence. Malton food scene generates posts.
Low presence — skip if profile uncertain or surname unknown
